message:
fix(mcp_config): broaden claude_desktop_config_path Unix branch beyond linux

`claude_desktop_config_path` previously gated the XDG / `~/.config`
fallback on `cfg(target_os = "linux")`. The same convention applies
to every non-macOS Unix (FreeBSD, OpenBSD, NetBSD, Illumos, etc.)
that ships Claude Desktop or a downstream client looking up the same
path — those targets returned `None` and downstream callers had no
way to discover the config.

Switch to `cfg(all(unix, not(target_os = "macos")))` so the XDG /
`~/.config/claude/config.json` resolution covers every Unix-but-not-
macOS target. macOS still uses the explicit `Claude/claude_desktop_
config.json` under `dirs::data_dir()`; Windows still uses its own
branch above.

Add a final `cfg(not(any(target_os = "macos", target_os = "windows",
unix)))` arm that returns `None` so the function is exhaustive on
truly exotic targets (wasm32, redox, etc.) and the compiler doesn't
emit a "no code reaches end of function" warning under the new gate.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.7 (1M context) <noreply@anthropic.com>
